sources.title: [PDF] Xofigo, INN- radium-223 dichloride
sources.snippet:
indicated for the treatment of adult patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC),
symptomatic bone metastases and no known visceral metastases, in progression after at least two prior lines ... Its active moiety radium-223 (as radium-223 dichloride) mimics calcium and selectively targets bone,
specifically areas of bone metastases, by forming complexes with the bone mineral hydroxyapatite.

sources.title: ARTBIO
sources.snippet: Radioligand therapy (RLT) has offered a promising new approach for cancer treatment. While beta radioligand therapies are currently more common in clinical practice, alpha radioligand therapies offer potentially significantly better efficacy and safety to patients. The ARTBIO approach is holistic. It is underpinned by a deep understanding of cancer biology, and the infrastructure needed to effectively design, manufacture, and distribute our therapies. Our patient-centric approach rests on four pillars:Differentiated pipeline Isotope production: We are identifying specific combinations of targets and drug molecules best suited for ART. Our ARTs have high affinity and selectivity in order to effectively kill tumors while sparing healthy tissues. ARTBIO is shaped by a long-standing scientific legacy with nearly a century of pioneering work in radiation therapy. Born out of groundbreaking research conducted at the University of Oslo and Norway's Radium Hospital - a research hub with lineage to Marie Curie - ARTBIO's roots in radiology and radiation therapy run deep. Our scientific founders, Roy Larsen and Øyvind Bruland, invented Xofigo (later acquired by Bayer via Algeta), the first-ever metabolically targeted alpha therapy. Both collaborated with the F-Prime team and Radforsk to lay the foundation of ARTBIO in 2021. Our team is composed of multi-disciplinary experts with decades of experience in advancing complex drugs through all stages of clinical development. We are driven by the power of collective thinking, diversity, and a passion to create smarter solutions together. Finally, we take pride in combining creativity and scientific expertise to address challenges. We value inclusion, collaboration, and fostering strong relationships with our team, partners, health care.

Target / Indication.display_value: Castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC) with symptomatic bone metastases and no known visceral metastases
Target / Indication.full_value: Castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC) with symptomatic bone metastases and no known visceral metastases

comment.original_value: Bone metastases from castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC)
comment.validator_explanation: FDA-approved indication: treatment of patients with castration-resistant prostate cancer, symptomatic bone metastases and no known visceral metastatic disease [1]. EMA indication: metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC), symptomatic bone metastases and no known visceral metastases [2]. Mechanism: Radium-223 mimics calcium and selectively targets bone, forming complexes with bone mineral hydroxyapatite at areas of increased bone turnover [2]. Original value was incomplete—missing visceral metastases restriction and specific mechanism details.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator correctly expanded the indication to include critical restrictions (symptomatic bone metastases, no visceral metastases) from FDA/EMA labels, making it more precise and complete.

sources.title: ARTBIO
sources.snippet: Radioligand therapy (RLT) has offered a promising new approach for cancer treatment. While beta radioligand therapies are currently more common in clinical practice, alpha radioligand therapies offer potentially significantly better efficacy and safety to patients. The ARTBIO approach is holistic. It is underpinned by a deep understanding of cancer biology, and the infrastructure needed to effectively design, manufacture, and distribute our therapies. Our patient-centric approach rests on four pillars:Differentiated pipeline Isotope production: We are identifying specific combinations of targets and drug molecules best suited for ART. Our ARTs have high affinity and selectivity in order to effectively kill tumors while sparing healthy tissues. ARTBIO is shaped by a long-standing scientific legacy with nearly a century of pioneering work in radiation therapy. Born out of groundbreaking research conducted at the University of Oslo and Norway's Radium Hospital - a research hub with lineage to Marie Curie - ARTBIO's roots in radiology and radiation therapy run deep. Our scientific founders, Roy Larsen and Øyvind Bruland, invented Xofigo (later acquired by Bayer via Algeta), the first-ever metabolically targeted alpha therapy. Both collaborated with the F-Prime team and Radforsk to lay the foundation of ARTBIO in 2021. Our team is composed of multi-disciplinary experts with decades of experience in advancing complex drugs through all stages of clinical development. We are driven by the power of collective thinking, diversity, and a passion to create smarter solutions together. Finally, we take pride in combining creativity and scientific expertise to address challenges. We value inclusion, collaboration, and fostering strong relationships with our team, partners, health care.

comment.validator_explanation: Confirmed as alpha-emitting radionuclide. FDA label states radium-223 is primarily an alpha emitter with 95.3% fraction of energy emitted as alpha-particles [1]. EMA documentation confirms Xofigo is a therapeutic alpha particle-emitting pharmaceutical with radium-223 dichloride as active moiety [2]. Current value is accurate and consistent with authoritative sources.

sources.title: Xofigo (Radium Ra 223 Dichloride) - American Health & Drug Benefits
sources.snippet: In May 2013, the FDA approved radium Ra 223 dichloride (Xofigo Injection [formerly known as Alpharadin]; Bayer HealthCare Pharmaceuticals) for the treatment of patients with castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC) plus symptomatic bone metastases and no known visceral metastatic disease. 12 In a prespecified interim analysis, radium Ra 223 dichloride (radium-223) combined with best standard of care demonstrated a significant 3.2-month (hazard ratio, 0.70; 95% confidence interval, 0.55-0.88; P = .00185) improvement in median OS compared with placebo plus best standard of care. 13 The improvement in OS was supported by a delay in time to the first symptomatic skeletal-related event favoring the radium-223 arm. 13

sources.title: Xofigo | European Medicines Agency (EMA)www.ema.europa.eu › Home › Medicines
sources.snippet: Xofigo is a radiopharmaceutical (a medicine containing a radioactive substance) that is used to treat adults with cancer of the prostate (a gland of the male reproductive system). Xofigo is used when castration (to stop the production of male hormones) by surgery or with medicines does not work, and when the cancer has spread to the bones (bone metastases) and is causing symptoms such as pain, but has not spread to other internal organs. It should be used only for patients who have had at least two previous treatments for prostate cancer or who cannot receive other treatments. Xofigo is used on its own or in combination with a medicine known as an ‘LHRH analogue’. Xofigo contains the active substance radium-223 dichloride. Xofigo can only be obtained with a prescription and should only be handled and given by someone who is authorised to use radioactive medicines and after evaluation of the patient by a qualified doctor. The dose of Xofigo is calculated to provide a specific dose of radioactivity based on the patient’s body weight. The medicine is slowly injected into a vein usually for up to one minute. Injections are repeated every 4 weeks for a total of 6 injections.

comment.validator_explanation: Xofigo is an alpha particle-emitting radioactive therapeutic agent [1]. No diagnostic imaging component identified in regulatory documents or clinical literature. Purely therapeutic radioligand therapy, not a theranostic pair.

Specific Biochemistry & Approach.display_value: Bone-seeking alpha-emitter with calcium-mimetic mechanism targeting hydroxyapatite at sites of increased bone turnover; alpha particles induce double-strand DNA breaks with range <100 micrometers limiting damage to surrounding normal tissue
Specific Biochemistry & Approach.full_value: Bone-seeking alpha-emitter with calcium-mimetic mechanism targeting hydroxyapatite at sites of increased bone turnover; alpha particles induce double-strand DNA breaks with range <100 micrometers limiting damage to surrounding normal tissue

comment.original_value: Bone-seeking alpha-emitter for targeted radiation to bone metastases
comment.validator_explanation: Original value was simplified. Enhanced with specific biochemical details: Radium-223 acts as calcium-mimetic forming complexes with bone mineral hydroxyapatite [3]. FDA confirms alpha particle range <100 micrometers limits damage to surrounding tissue [1]. Mechanism involves induction of double-strand DNA breaks [1]. EMA notes high linear energy transfer of alpha emitters (80 keV/µm) leads to high frequency of double-strand DNA breaks [2]. No unique chelator platform (e.g., Macropa, Trillium) identified for Xofigo—it uses simple dichloride formulation, distinguishing it from newer targeted approaches.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator's detailed biochemical explanation is accurate and significantly enhances the original simplified description. It correctly specifies the calcium-mimetic mechanism, hydroxyapatite targeting, DNA break induction, and limited tissue penetration range from authoritative sources.

sources.title: ASCO 2025: XOFIGO® (radium-223 dichloride) Combination Data Showcases Clinical Benefits in Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer with Bone Metastases
sources.snippet:

general_notes: This table provides a comprehensive competitive landscape analysis of theranostic radiopharmaceutical products targeting cancer, with a focus on Ratio Therapeutics and its competitors. The goal is to identify 100+ unique products in the theranostic space, including Ratio's own pipeline. Theranostics involve paired diagnostic and therapeutic agents using radionuclides for cancer imaging and treatment. Key sources include clinical trial registries (ClinicalTrials.gov), company pipeline pages, FDA/EMA approvals, and industry databases. The global theranostic market is projected to grow from $2.66B in 2026 to $4.46B by 2031 (10.89% CAGR), driven by alpha-emitters (Ac-225), PSMA/SSTR2 therapies, and major pharma deals. Validate all information using official company press releases, investor presentations, clinical trial records, and reputable industry sources. Prioritize current information (last 2-3 years) and ensure entries are unique and exhaustive.
